mods.abstract: On Huangshiya, p. 104-105: Tokiwa first gives a description of the location of Yellow Stone Cliff. Then follows a description of the sculptures inside the grotto. This is his main concern; he does not write about the other niches. He says that among the many votive inscriptions at the site, three would refer to the sculptures inside the grotto. He then quotes a few characters of the following votive inscriptions: 孝昌三年 敬造石窟 (HSY 4) respectfully made [a] stone caves in the third year of the xiaochang era 元象二[he misreads 六]年造弥勒像 (HSY 7) made one Maitreya image in the second year of the yuanxiang era 興和二年 刻弥勒石像三軀 (HSY 8) carved three stone images of Maitreya in the second year of the xinghe era. Therefore, he concludes, the figures inside the grotto already existed in third year of the xiaochang era, 527.
